member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.Browser.BrowseAsync(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Opc.Ua.NodeId},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: 1. if, during a Browse or BrowseNext, service call one of the status codes BadNoContinuationPoint or BadContinuationPointInvalid is returned, the node is browsed again. 2. tries to avoid the status code BadNoContinuationPoint by creating packages of size at most MaxNodesPerBrowse, taken from the Operationlimits retrieved from the server at client startup. 3. with the help of a new property of the session the new method calls Browse for at most min(MaxNodesPerBrowse, MaxBrowseContinuationPoints) nodes in one call, to further reduce the risk of the status code BadNoContinuationPoint 4. calls BrowseNext, if necessary, before working on a new package. This is the reason that the packages have to be created directly in this method and package creation cannot be delegated to the SessionClientBatched class. This call sequence avoids the cannibalization of continuation points from previously worked on packages, at least if no concurrent browse operation is started. (The server is supposed to manage the continuation point quota on session level.The reference server, which is used in the tests, does this correctly). 5. the maximum number of browse continuation points is retrieved from the server capabilities in a new method, which may also be viewed as a prototype for evaluation of all server capabilities (this is not an operation limit).
member.param: The nodes to browse
Cancellation token to use

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.Browser.BrowseAsync(Opc.Ua.ISessionClient,Opc.Ua.RequestHeader,Opc.Ua.ViewDescription,System.Collections.Generic.List{Opc.Ua.NodeId},System.UInt32,Opc.Ua.BrowseDirection,Opc.Ua.NodeId,System.Boolean,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Call the browse service asynchronously and call browse next, if applicable, immediately afterwards. Observe proper treatment of specific service results, specifically BadNoContinuationPoint and BadContinuationPointInvalid

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions
member.summary: Options for controlling NodeSet export behavior.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions.ExportValues
member.summary: Whether to export value elements for variables. Default is false (values are only exported for Complete option).

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions.ExportParentNodeId
member.summary: Whether to export the ParentNodeId attribute. Default is false (ParentNodeId is redundant as it can be inferred from references).

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions.ExportUserContext
member.summary: Whether to export user context attributes (UserAccessLevel, UserExecutable, UserWriteMask, UserRolePermissions). Default is false (user context attributes are not exported). When true, UserAccessLevel is only exported if it differs from AccessLevel.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions.Default
member.summary: Gets the default export options (no values, essential attributes only). This produces minimal file size suitable for schema definitions. MethodDeclarationId is always exported. User context attributes are not exported.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.NodeSetExportOptions.Complete
member.summary: Gets complete export options with all metadata and values. Use this for full data export including runtime values and user context attributes. MethodDeclarationId is always exported. UserAccessLevel is only exported when different from AccessLevel.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.NamespaceDoc
member.summary: The Opc.Ua.Client namespace defines classes which can be used to implement a UA client. These classes manage client side state information, provide higher level abstractions for UA tasks such as managing sessions/subscriptions and saving/restoring connection information for later use.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.LruNodeCache
member.summary: This node cache is a real cache and consists of three LRU caches. These caches contain nodes and references (Read and browse). Cache entries expire after not being accessed for a certain time. The cache is thread-safe and can be accessed by multiple readers. It is also limited to a capacity at which point least recently used entries will be evicted.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.ReverseConnectManager
member.summary: The implementation of a reverse connect client manager.
member.remarks: This reverse connect manager allows to register for reverse connections with various strategies: i) take any connection. ii) filter for a specific application Uri and Url scheme. iii) filter for the Url. Second, any filter can be combined with the Once or Always flag.

member.name: F:Opc.Ua.Client.ReverseConnectManager.DefaultWaitTimeout
member.summary: A default value for reverse hello configurations, if undefined.
member.remarks: This value is used as wait timeout if the value is undefined by a caller.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession
member.summary: Manages a session with a server.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.KeepAlive
member.summary: Raised when a keep alive arrives from the server or an error is detected.
member.remarks: Once a session is created a timer will periodically read the server state and current time. If this read operation succeeds this event will be raised each time the keep alive period elapses. If an error is detected (KeepAliveStopped == true) then this event will be raised as well.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.Notification
member.summary: Raised when a notification message arrives in a publish response.
member.remarks: All publish requests are managed by the Session object. When a response arrives it is validated and passed to the appropriate Subscription object and this event is raised.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.PublishError
member.summary: Raised when an exception occurs while processing a publish response.
member.remarks: Exceptions in a publish response are not necessarily fatal and the Session will attempt to recover by issuing Republish requests if missing messages are detected. That said, timeout errors may be a symptom of a OperationTimeout that is too short when compared to the shortest PublishingInterval/KeepAliveCount amount the current Subscriptions. The OperationTimeout should be twice the minimum value for PublishingInterval*KeepAliveCount.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.PublishSequenceNumbersToAcknowledge
member.summary: Raised when a publish request is about to acknowledge sequence numbers.
member.remarks: If the client chooses to defer acknowledge of sequence numbers, it is responsible to transfer these to the deferred list.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SubscriptionsChanged
member.summary: Raised when a subscription is added or removed

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SessionClosing
member.summary: Raised to indicate the session is closing.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SessionConfigurationChanged
member.summary: Raised to indicate the session configuration changed.
member.remarks: An example for a session configuration change is a new user identity, a new server nonce, a new locale etc.

member.summary: Gets the name assigned to the session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SessionTimeout
member.summary: Gets the period for wich the server will maintain the session if there is no communication from the client.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.Handle

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.ServerUris

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SystemContext
member.summary: Gets the system context for use with the session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.Factory
member.summary: Gets the factory used to create encodeable objects that the server understands.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.TypeTree
member.summary: Gets the cache of the server's type tree.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.NodeCache
member.summary: Gets the cache of nodes fetched from the server.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.FilterContext
member.summary: Gets the context to use for filter operations.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.PreferredLocales
member.summary: Gets the locales that the server should use when returning localized text.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.Subscriptions
member.summary: Gets the subscriptions owned by the session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.SubscriptionCount
member.summary: Gets the number of subscriptions owned by the session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.DeleteSubscriptionsOnClose
member.summary: If the subscriptions are deleted when a session is closed.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.PublishRequestCancelDelayOnCloseSession
member.summary: Gets or sets the time in milliseconds to wait for outstanding publish requests to complete before canceling them during session close.
member.remarks: A value of 0 means no waiting - outstanding requests are canceled immediately. A negative value means wait indefinitely for all outstanding requests to complete. The default value is 5000 milliseconds (5 seconds).

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.DefaultSubscription
member.summary: Gets or Sets the default subscription for the session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.KeepAliveInterval
member.summary: Gets or Sets how frequently the server is pinged to see if communication is still working.
member.remarks: This interval controls how much time elaspes before a communication error is detected. If everything is ok the KeepAlive event will be raised each time this period elapses.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.KeepAliveStopped
member.summary: Returns true if the session is not receiving keep alives.
member.remarks: Set to true if the server does not respond for the KeepAliveInterval times a configurable factor + a configurable guard band. Set to false is communication recovers.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.LastKeepAliveTime
member.summary: Gets the time of the last keep alive. This time may not be monotonic if the system time is changed.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.ISession.LastKeepAliveTickCount
member.summary: Gets the TickCount in ms of the last keep alive based on .

member.summary: Constructs a new instance of the class.

member.summary: Constructs a new instance of the class.
member.param: The channel used to communicate with the server.
The configuration for the client application.
The endpoint used to initialize the channel.
The certificate to use for the client.
The certificate chain of the client certificate.
The list of available endpoints returned by server in GetEndpoints() response.
The value of profileUris used in GetEndpoints() request.
member.remarks: The application configuration is used to look up the certificate if none is provided. The clientCertificate must have the private key. This will require that the certificate be loaded from a certicate store. Converting a DER encoded blob to a X509Certificate2 will not include a private key. The availableEndpoints and discoveryProfileUris parameters are used to validate that the list of EndpointDescriptions returned at GetEndpoints matches the list returned at CreateSession.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Closes the session and the underlying channel.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.KeepAlive
member.summary: Raised when a keep alive arrives from the server or an error is detected.
member.remarks: Once a session is created a timer will periodically read the server state and current time. If this read operation succeeds this event will be raised each time the keep alive period elapses. If an error is detected (KeepAliveStopped == true) then this event will be raised as well.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.Notification
member.summary: Raised when a notification message arrives in a publish response.
member.remarks: All publish requests are managed by the Session object. When a response arrives it is validated and passed to the appropriate Subscription object and this event is raised.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.PublishError
member.summary: Raised when an exception occurs while processing a publish response.
member.remarks: Exceptions in a publish response are not necessarily fatal and the Session will attempt to recover by issuing Republish requests if missing messages are detected. That said, timeout errors may be a symptom of a OperationTimeout that is too short when compared to the shortest PublishingInterval/KeepAliveCount amount the current Subscriptions. The OperationTimeout should be twice the minimum value for PublishingInterval*KeepAliveCount.

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.SubscriptionsChanged
member.summary: Raised when a subscription is added or removed

member.name: E:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.SessionClosing
member.summary: Raised to indicate the session is closing.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.KeepAliveInterval
member.summary: Gets or Sets how frequently the server is pinged to see if communication is still working.
member.remarks: This interval controls how much time elaspes before a communication error is detected. If everything is ok the KeepAlive event will be raised each time this period elapses.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Session.KeepAliveStopped
member.summary: Returns true if the session is not receiving keep alives.
member.remarks: Set to true if the server does not respond for the KeepAliveInterval * 1 (KeepAliveIntervalFactor) + 1 Second (KeepAliveGuardBand) * To change the sensitivity of the keep alive check, set the / fields. or if another error was reported. Set to false is communication is ok or recovered.

member.summary: Represents the event arguments provided when publish response sequence numbers are about to be ackknowledged with a publish request.
member.remarks: A callee can defer an acknowledge to the next publish request by moving the to the deferred list. The callee can modify the list of acknowledgements to send, it is the responsibility of the caller to protect the lists for modifications.

member.summary: The acknowledgements which are sent with the next publish request.
member.remarks: A client may also choose to remove an acknowledgement from this list to add it back to the list in a subsequent callback when the request is fully processed.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.PublishSequenceNumbersToAcknowledgeEventArgs.DeferredAcknowledgementsToSend
member.summary: The deferred list of acknowledgements.
member.remarks: The callee can transfer an outstanding to this list to defer the acknowledge of a sequence number to the next publish request.

member.summary: Reads a byte string value safely in fragments if needed. Uses the byte string size limits to chunk the reads if needed. The node id of a byte string variable
A chunk size to enforce
Cancellation token to cancel operation with

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions
member.summary:
Serializable options for a client monitored item.
These options map to parameters used when creating and modifying monitored items within a subscription. See OPC UA Part4 v1.05 Sections 5.13 and 5.14 (Subscription / MonitoredItem Service Sets): https://reference.opcfoundation.org/Core/Part4/v105/docs/5.13 and https://reference.opcfoundation.org/Core/Part4/v105/docs/5.14.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.DisplayName
member.summary:
Local human readable display name used by the client for logging and diagnostics of the monitored item. This is not the DisplayName attribute of the target node and is not sent in the CreateMonitoredItems request. Choose a name that reflects the business purpose (e.g. "Tank1.Level").
Spec Context: Client side metadata.
Reference: Part4 Section5.13.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.StartNodeId
member.summary:
Starting NodeId used with RelativePath to resolve the target node. If RelativePath is null the StartNodeId is the node directly monitored. This corresponds to the nodeId when constructed after path resolution. NodeId.Null indicates it is not set yet.
Spec: ReadValueId.nodeId.
Reference: Part4 Section5.13.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.RelativePath
member.summary: A relative browse path (client side string form) from StartNodeId to the final target node or attribute to monitor. When supplied the client resolves it to a NodeId using Browse / TranslateBrowsePaths services before creating the monitored item. Null means no path; monitor the StartNodeId directly.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.NodeClass
member.summary: The expected NodeClass of the target node (Variable, Object, etc.). Primarily used for client validation after resolving the NodeId. Monitoring Variables yields DataChange notifications; monitoring Events uses Object / View and an EventFilter. Ensuring the correct NodeClass helps avoid invalid monitored item creation requests.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.AttributeId
member.summary: The AttributeId to monitor on the target node. For data changes this is typically Attributes.Value; for Events the attribute may be Attributes.EventNotifier; for other use cases StatusCode or Timestamp attributes may be monitored. Maps to ReadValueId.AttributeId in the service request.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.IndexRange
member.summary: IndexRange selecting a subset of an array or matrix value (e.g. "0:9" for first10 elements). If null the entire value is monitored. This corresponds to ReadValueId.indexRange and is applied by the server when generating notifications, reducing bandwidth for large arrays.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.Encoding
member.summary: Requested data encoding (QualifiedName) for complex values (e.g. specific DataTypeEncoding). This maps to ReadValueId.dataEncoding. Use QualifiedName.Null for default encoding. Ensures notifications are serialized in a form understood by the client.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.MonitoringMode
member.summary: Requested MonitoringMode for the item: Disabled (no sampling), Sampling (server samples but does not queue notifications), Reporting (samples and queues notifications for Publish). This value may be changed later via SetMonitoringMode. Default is Reporting for typical data collection.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.SamplingInterval
member.summary: Requested samplingInterval (ms) for the server's data sampling of the underlying value. A value of -1 requests the server default. The actual revised interval may differ; use the created MonitoredItem's server-revised value for final timing. Very small intervals increase load; very large intervals reduce data freshness.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.Filter
member.summary:
Optional server side filter controlling which data changes or events generate notifications. For Variables use DataChangeFilter or AggregateFilter; for Events use EventFilter. Null means no additional filtering beyond sampling and queueing. Proper filters reduce bandwidth and client processing.
Spec: MonitoringFilter types (DataChangeFilter, EventFilter, AggregateFilter).
Reference: Part4 Section5.13.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.QueueSize
member.summary: Requested queueSize specifying the maximum number of notifications the server retains for this item between Publish responses.0 (or 1 depending on server) means minimal queue. Larger queues reduce risk of data loss during short client delays but increase memory usage. Maps to requestedParameters.queueSize. The server may revise.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemOptions.DiscardOldest
member.summary: discardOldest policy: if true the server discards the oldest entry when the queue is full and a new notification arrives; if false it discards the newest notification. The usual recommendation is true for streaming latest-value scenarios; false preserves the earliest samples for batch integrity.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemState
member.summary: State object that is used for snapshotting the monitored item

member.summary: Server-side identifier assigned to this monitored item (the monitoredItemId). Stored so the client can correlate notifications and perform Modify/SetMonitoringMode/Delete operations across reconnects. 0 indicates not yet created or invalidated.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemState.ClientId
member.summary: Client-assigned handle used in Publish notifications (clientHandle) to quickly map incoming data changes or events to local application structures without lookups on serverId. Should be unique per subscription. Typically used as an index/key into client data structures.

member.summary: When the state was created.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemState.TriggeringItemId
member.summary: Server-side identifier of the triggering item if this monitored item is triggered by another item. 0 indicates this item is not triggered. Used to restore triggering links after session reconnect.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.MonitoredItemState.TriggeredItems
member.summary: Collection of server-side identifiers of monitored items that are triggered by this item. Empty or null if this item does not trigger any other items. Used to restore triggering links after session reconnect.

member.summary: The minimum lifetime for subscriptions in milliseconds.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.DisableMonitoredItemCache
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the notifications are cached within the monitored items.
member.value: true if monitored item cache is disabled; otherwise, false.
member.remarks: Applications must process the Session.Notication event if this is set to true. This flag improves performance by eliminating the processing involved in updating the cache.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.SequentialPublishing
member.summary: Gets or sets the behavior of waiting for sequential order in handling incoming messages.
member.value: true if incoming messages are handled sequentially; false otherwise.
member.remarks: Setting to true means incoming messages are processed in a "single-threaded" manner and callbacks will not be invoked in parallel.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.RepublishAfterTransfer
member.summary: If the available sequence numbers of a subscription are republished or acknowledged after a transfer.
member.remarks: Default false, set to true if no data loss is important and available publish requests (sequence numbers) that were never acknowledged should be recovered with a republish. The setting is used after a subscription transfer.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.TransferId
member.summary: The unique identifier assigned by the server which can be used to transfer a session.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.FastDataChangeCallback
member.summary: Gets or sets the fast data change callback.
member.remarks: Only one callback is allowed at a time but it is more efficient to call than an event.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.Subscription.FastEventCallback
member.summary: Gets or sets the fast event callback.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions
member.summary:
Serializable options for a subscription.
These client side options correspond to parameters used with the CreateSubscription / ModifySubscription and related services in the OPC UA Subscription Service Set. See OPC UA Part4 v1.05 Sections 5.13 and 5.14: https://reference.opcfoundation.org/Core/Part4/v105/docs/5.13 and https://reference.opcfoundation.org/Core/Part4/v105/docs/5.14.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.DisplayName
member.summary: A human readable display name for the subscription instance used locally by the client for logging, diagnostics and UI. This value is not sent to the server as part of the service parameters; it is purely client side metadata. Choose a name that helps operators identify the purpose of the subscription (e.g. "ProcessValues", "Alarms").

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.PublishingInterval
member.summary: Requested publishingInterval (ms) sent in CreateSubscription. It defines the cyclic interval at which the server evaluates the subscription for notifications and prepares the next Publish response. The server may revise this value; the effective revised value is available via the Subscription object after creation. A value <=0 falls back to the server default. Selecting too small an interval can increase CPU and network load.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.KeepAliveCount
member.summary: Requested keepAliveCount (number of publishing intervals) determining how many consecutive publishing cycles may pass with no notifications before the server sends a KeepAlive Publish response. Lower values mean the client gets more frequent confirmation that the subscription is alive even in quiet periods. The server may revise this.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.LifetimeCount
member.summary: Requested lifetimeCount (number of publishing intervals) used by the server to detect client inactivity. If the server does not receive Publish requests for lifetimeCount consecutive intervals the subscription may be terminated. Must be larger than keepAliveCount (spec recommends at least3x). The server may revise this value.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.MaxNotificationsPerPublish
member.summary: Requested maxNotificationsPerPublish limiting the number of notifications returned in a single Publish response to prevent oversized messages.0 means server default (usually unlimited or some internal cap). The server may revise. Tune this to balance latency versus network packet size and memory consumption. Large bursts get split across multiple Publish responses if necessary.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.PublishingEnabled
member.summary: Requested publishingEnabled state. If false the server creates the subscription but does not send notifications until it is later enabled with ModifySubscription or SetPublishingMode. Useful for staging monitored items.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.Priority
member.summary: Requested priority hint allowing the server to schedule higher priority subscriptions first when resources are constrained. OPC UA servers may use this value as a relative ranking (higher = more important) but are not required to strictly enforce ordering.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.TimestampsToReturn
member.summary: Which timestamps (Source / Server / Both / Neither) the client requests in notifications. This maps to the TimestampsToReturn enum used in the MonitoredItem and Read services. The default of Both provides maximum context at the cost of a few extra bytes.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.MaxMessageCount
member.summary: Maximum number of Publish responses cached locally by the client for late processing or sequence gap recovery. This is a client side buffering control (not part of wire services). Larger values allow more tolerance to temporary processing delays; smaller values reduce memory usage.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.MinLifetimeInterval
member.summary: A client side min interval (ms) used to derive a safe lifetimeCount when constructing subscription requests. Helps enforce policy such that lifetimeCount * publishingInterval is not below an application defined minimum lifetime.0 means disabled.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.DisableMonitoredItemCache
member.summary: When true the client disables its per-monitored-item value cache, potentially improving throughput for high frequency data changes at the cost of losing last-value lookups without application tracking. Use for streaming scenarios where each notification is processed once then discarded.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.SequentialPublishing
member.summary: When true incoming Publish responses are processed strictly sequentially in the order of their sequence numbers, even if multiple arrive concurrently. This can simplify application logic for ordering dependent processing (e.g. aggregate calculations) at the cost of reduced parallelism.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.RepublishAfterTransfer
member.summary: When true the client will automatically issue Republish requests after a TransferSubscription or other recovery scenario to obtain any available lost sequence numbers and minimize data loss. This automates the gap recovery behavior defined for the Republish service.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.SubscriptionOptions.TransferId
member.summary: The transferable subscription identifier (server assigned) used to reattach a subscription to a new session via TransferSubscriptions. 0 indicates no server transfer id known or the subscription is not currently transferable. Persisting this value allows restoring behavior after reconnect.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncAutoResetEvent
member.summary: An async-compatible auto-reset event.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncAutoResetEvent.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates an async-compatible auto-reset event.
member.param: Whether the auto-reset event is initially set or unset.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncAutoResetEvent.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an async-compatible auto-reset event that is initially unset.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncAutoResetEvent.WaitAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously waits for this event to be set. If the event is set, this method will auto-reset it and return immediately, even if the cancellation token is already signalled. If the wait is canceled, then it will not auto-reset this event.
member.param: The cancellation token used to cancel this wait.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncAutoResetEvent.Set
member.summary: Sets the event, atomically completing a task returned by WaitAsync. If the event is already set, this method does nothing.

member.name: T:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent
member.summary: An async-compatible manual-reset event.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates an async-compatible manual-reset event.
member.param: Whether the manual-reset event is initially set or unset.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.#ctor
member.summary: Creates an async-compatible manual-reset event that is initially unset.

member.name: P:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.IsSet
member.summary: Whether this event is currently set. This member is seldom used; code using this member has a high possibility of race conditions.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.WaitAsync
member.summary: Asynchronously waits for this event to be set.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.WaitAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Asynchronously waits for this event to be set or for the wait to be canceled.
member.param: The cancellation token used to cancel the wait. If this token is already canceled, this method will first check whether the event is set.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.Set
member.summary: Sets the event, atomically completing every task returned WaitAsync. If the event is already set, this method does nothing.

member.name: M:Opc.Ua.Client.AsyncManualResetEvent.Reset
member.summary: Resets the event. If the event is already reset, this method does nothing.

member.remarks: Assigns the batched collection result to the result if the result collection is not initialized, adds the range to the result collections otherwise. The string table indexes are updated in the diagnostic infos if necessary.
